comments.body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Persisted policy merge currently suppresses explicit `start` policy flags.**

At Line 311, persisted `runPolicy` is always merged into `context.config` when present. For `start` with explicit flags, Line 351 re-stamps `state.runPolicy` from `resolvedConfig`, but `startTicket(...)` still receives `context.config` (already overwritten by persisted policy), so this invocation can run with stale policy values.




<details>
<summary>Suggested fix</summary>

```diff
+    const hasExplicitPolicyFlags =
+      parsed.boundaryMode !== undefined ||
+      parsed.subagentReviewPolicy !== undefined ||
+      parsed.prReviewPolicy !== undefined ||
+      parsed.reviewSubagent !== undefined ||
+      parsed.sameReviewSubagent === true;
+
     // Apply persisted runPolicy so all downstream call sites use the governing
     // policy values from state, not the current config.
-    if (hadPersistedRunPolicy && state.runPolicy != null) {
+    if (
+      hadPersistedRunPolicy &&
+      state.runPolicy != null &&
+      !(parsed.command === 'start' && hasExplicitPolicyFlags)
+    ) {
       context = {
         ...context,
         config: applyRunPolicyToConfig(context.config, state.runPolicy),
       };
     }
@@
-        const hasExplicitPolicyFlags =
-          parsed.boundaryMode !== undefined ||
-          parsed.subagentReviewPolicy !== undefined ||
-          parsed.prReviewPolicy !== undefined ||
-          parsed.reviewSubagent !== undefined ||
-          parsed.sameReviewSubagent === true;
         const stateForStart = hasExplicitPolicyFlags
           ? { ...state, runPolicy: deriveRunPolicyFromConfig(resolvedConfig) }
           : state;
```
</details>


Also applies to: 344-352

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@tools/delivery/cli-runner.ts` around lines 311 - 318, Persisted runPolicy is
being merged into context.config unconditionally which overwrites explicit start
flags; update the merge so explicit start flags win: either (a) only apply
applyRunPolicyToConfig when the action is not start (use hadPersistedRunPolicy
and the command), or (b) when handling start, compute a merged config that
applies state.runPolicy but then re-apply/override with resolvedConfig (or the
explicit start flags from resolvedConfig) before calling startTicket; reference
applyRunPolicyToConfig, context.config, state.runPolicy, resolvedConfig and
startTicket and ensure the config passed into startTicket contains the explicit
start flags from resolvedConfig rather than the raw persisted values.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:poseidon:hawk -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

_🛠️ Refactor suggestion_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Add one regression test for precedence in `runDeliveryOrchestrator` (`start` + explicit flags + persisted policy).**

This suite covers the pure helper well, but it doesn’t guard the wiring edge where persisted policy can override explicit `start` flags during the same invocation.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@tools/delivery/test/p8-01.test.ts` around lines 36 - 73, Add a regression
test in the same suite that exercises the wiring in runDeliveryOrchestrator:
invoke runDeliveryOrchestrator (or the exported start entrypoint it uses) with a
persisted RunPolicy (PERSISTED_RUN_POLICY) and explicit start flags that
conflict with the persisted policy, and assert the correct precedence (explicit
start flags should win over persisted policy or vice versa according to expected
behavior). Target the same fields covered by applyRunPolicyToConfig (e.g.,
ticketBoundaryMode, reviewPolicy.subagentReview, reviewSubagentOverride) so the
test verifies the end-to-end precedence when start + explicit flags + persisted
policy are supplied in one invocation. Ensure the test sets up mocks/stubs the
same way existing tests do and asserts the final orchestrator config values
returned or passed to downstream code.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:poseidon:hawk -->

[![Review Change Stack](https://storage.googleapis.com/coderabbit_public_assets/review-stack-in-coderabbit-ui.svg)](https://app.coderabbit.ai/change-stack/cesarnml/son-of-anton/pull/28)

<!-- review_stack_entry_end -->
<!-- walkthrough_start -->

## Walkthrough

This PR implements run policy consumer wiring for the P8.01 delivery phase. It introduces `LoadStateResult` as an explicit type contract for the `loadState` function, adds `applyRunPolicyToConfig` helper in `tools/delivery/state.ts` to overlay persisted policy onto orchestrator config with conditional field mapping, and integrates this into `runDeliveryOrchestrator` by loading state, detecting persisted policy, and reassigning context config with the applied policy. Comprehensive test coverage validates the policy override behavior, and documentation confirms specification compliance.

<!-- walkthrough_end -->
<!-- tips_start -->

```
Inline comments:
In `@tools/delivery/cli-runner.ts`:
- Around line 311-318: Persisted runPolicy is being merged into context.config
unconditionally which overwrites explicit start flags; update the merge so
explicit start flags win: either (a) only apply applyRunPolicyToConfig when the
action is not start (use hadPersistedRunPolicy and the command), or (b) when
handling start, compute a merged config that applies state.runPolicy but then
re-apply/override with resolvedConfig (or the explicit start flags from
resolvedConfig) before calling startTicket; reference applyRunPolicyToConfig,
context.config, state.runPolicy, resolvedConfig and startTicket and ensure the
config passed into startTicket contains the explicit start flags from
resolvedConfig rather than the raw persisted values.

In `@tools/delivery/test/p8-01.test.ts`:
- Around line 36-73: Add a regression test in the same suite that exercises the
wiring in runDeliveryOrchestrator: invoke runDeliveryOrchestrator (or the
exported start entrypoint it uses) with a persisted RunPolicy
(PERSISTED_RUN_POLICY) and explicit start flags that conflict with the persisted
policy, and assert the correct precedence (explicit start flags should win over
persisted policy or vice versa according to expected behavior). Target the same
fields covered by applyRunPolicyToConfig (e.g., ticketBoundaryMode,
reviewPolicy.subagentReview, reviewSubagentOverride) so the test verifies the
end-to-end precedence when start + explicit flags + persisted policy are
supplied in one invocation. Ensure the test sets up mocks/stubs the same way
existing tests do and asserts the final orchestrator config values returned or

```
